summary: Updated the durable workshop website spec to match the current homepage information architecture after Builder removed the motivation and differentiation sections and added schedule, submissions, and contact placeholders.
work_completed: Reviewed `.control-tower/packets/inbox/builder-f99f0194-2bb8-41d6-a9cc-f622e2027723-result.json` alongside `index.html` and `assets/main.css` before editing docs.
Updated `docs/product-specs/workshop-website.md` so the Current Behavior section matches the live homepage sections and five-link in-page navigation.
Refined the spec's content rules and near-term follow-ups to reflect placeholder schedule, submissions, and contact content rather than future hypothetical sections.

findings.message: No new durable doc page was needed; the existing workshop website spec covered the change.
Docs indices did not require refresh because no new documentation files were added.

Confirmed schedule details, submission links, and organizer/contact information remain unresolved in both implementation and docs; the spec intentionally preserves placeholder status instead of inventing details.

follow_up_recommendations: Replace the placeholder schedule, submissions, and contact text in both `index.html` and `docs/product-specs/workshop-website.md` once logistics are confirmed.
If the homepage grows beyond a single page or adds external submission/contact services, update `docs/design-docs/site-architecture.md` or add a runbook describing those dependencies.
memory_worthy: Homepage IA now consists of Overview, Scope, Schedule, Submissions, and Contact sections; motivation and differentiation are no longer part of the public site spec.
